query: Has Russia announced any extension of its gasoline (motor gasoline/benzine) export ban beyond July 31, 2026? Look for any cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June 2026 or early July 2026 reported by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. Also describe the recent status of Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries and domestic gasoline shortages as of late June 2026, and historically how many days before each deadline past gasoline export ban extensions were formally announced.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings:

**Has an extension beyond July 31, 2026 been announced? — No.** As of the latest reporting (through July 3, 2026), no cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement extending the gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 has been issued. The current full ban (all exporters including producers), set by the April 2, 2026 decree, remains legally due to expire July 31, 2026.

**What officials were actually discussing (late June 2026):** A *separate* potential ban on **diesel** exports by producers (Novak, June 23–26), citing a diesel surplus. Russia held off on that decision June 28 to reassess. The gasoline ban was not the subject of an extension announcement.

**Drone strikes & shortages (late June 2026):** ~25% of Russian refining capacity disabled; Moscow, Krasnodar, and Yaroslavl refineries struck; Putin publicly admitted shortages June 28 ("lines at gas stations") while calling them "not critical"; nationwide rationing (20–30 L/vehicle); Crimea state of emergency; petrol imports from India and plans for ~400,000 t/month.

**Historical timing of extensions:** Russia typically formalizes extensions within ~1–5 days of the deadline — e.g., the Dec 31, 2025 ban was extended Dec 27, 2025 (~4 days prior), and the Sep 30, 2025 decree was signed ~1 day before its Oct 1 effective date. This implies any July 31, 2026 extension would most likely be enacted in the final days of July — after the current research cutoff.

sources.title: Ban on gasoline exports from Russia extended to producers of petroleum products
sources.snippet: The Russian government has extended gasoline export restrictions effective until July 31, 2026, to direct producers of petroleum products. The corresponding decree has been signed.

sources.title: Russia Bans Producers From Exporting Gasoline Until July 31
sources.snippet: A new decree extends the ban to producers, with exemptions granted only under intergovernmental agreements. Deadline July 31, 2026.

sources.title: Russian authorities considering ban on diesel exports by producers for several months
sources.snippet: Novak: We have a surplus of diesel; a few months are sufficient to supply the domestic market in greater volume and replenish stocks.

sources.title: Russian deputy PM Novak to hold meeting on fuel market, diesel export ban to be discussed
sources.snippet: Novak will hold a meeting where the possibility of banning diesel exports for producers will be considered.

sources.title: Putin Acknowledges Russia Faces Fuel Supply Problems; Russia Holds Off on Diesel Export Ban
sources.snippet: Russia holds off on diesel export ban to reassess Monday.

sources.title: Putin's fuel shortage admission signals growing strain on Russia's energy infrastructure
sources.snippet: Putin conceded fuel shortages following long-range Ukrainian drone strikes; described as temporary deficit; refineries in Krasnodar and Yaroslavl struck.

sources.title: 'The crisis is deep': The view from Russia as fuel shortages worsen
sources.snippet: ~25% of refining capacity disabled; rationing to 20-30 litres; petrol imports from India; no mention of extension beyond July 31 2026.

sources.title: Russia Considers Diesel Export Ban as Fuel Market Pressures Mount
sources.snippet: Russia considering a full ban on diesel exports amid refinery disruptions and shortages linked to Ukrainian attacks.

sources.title: Russia Extends Gasoline Export Ban, Restricts Diesel Shipments
sources.snippet: Sept 30, 2025 decree prolongs gasoline export ban until Dec 31, applying to all exporters including producers.

sources.title: Russia Extends Temporary Gasoline-Export Ban Through February
sources.snippet: Dec 27, 2025: ban extended to Feb 28, 2026 for all exporters including producers.

sources.title: Russia to Allow Gasoline Exports for Producers Through July
sources.snippet: Jan 31, 2026: Russia eases ban, allowing producers to export gasoline through July 31.

sources.title: Russia Bans Jet Fuel Exports Until Late November
sources.snippet: Jet fuel export ban introduced June 1 until end of November 2026 (government.ru/news/58885).

sources.title: 2025–2026 Russian fuel crisis
sources.snippet: Crisis escalated mid-2026; Crimea declared state of emergency in June and banned fuel sales.

sources.title: Putin shrugs off fuel shortages in Russia as he ramps up attacks on Ukraine
sources.snippet: Putin dismissed fuel shortages as 'not critical' despite severe shortages across Russia (July 3, 2026).

sources.title: Russia Weighs Extending Gasoline Export Ban
sources.snippet: TASS reported Russia could extend the ban from the Dec 31, 2025 end-date to end of February.
